sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Good progress! The pair moved from cols 12-13/14-17 to cols 20-21/22-25. The lower white region expanded from cols 0-11 to cols 0-19.
Now the pair's N strip is at cols 20-21, and the target is at cols 28-29. I need 2 more expansions to reach cols 28-29.
But wait, the lower white region is at cols 0-19 (20 columns). The maximum seems to be cols 0-23 (24 columns), based on previous observations. So I can expand 1 more time to reach cols 0-23, which would put the pair at cols 24-25/26-29.
But the target is at cols 28-29, and the pair's N strip would be at cols 24-25. That's still 4 columns short.
Hmm, but the top white region is at cols 0-51 (52 columns). Half of 52 is 26, which would be cols 0-25. So the lower white region's maximum might be cols 0-25 (26 columns), which would put the pair at cols 26-27/28-29. That would align the pair's N strip with cols 26-27 and the c blob with cols 28-29.
Wait, but the target is at cols 28-29, and the pair's c blob would be at cols 28-29. So the c blob would align with the target.
Let me try expanding 2 more times to see if the lower white region can reach cols 0-25.
Actually, let me think about this more carefully. The lower white region is at cols 0-19 (20 columns). Each expansion adds 4 columns, so:
- 1 expansion: cols 0-23 (24 columns), pair at cols 24-25/26-29
- 2 expansions: cols 0-27 (28 columns), pair at cols 28-29/30-33
But the maximum might be cols 0-23 (24 columns), based on previous observations. Let me try expanding 2 more times and see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# Expand 2 more times
predict(expect_change=True, note='Expand 2 more: pair from cols 20 to cols 28')
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 2},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': 2}
])

print("Result:", last_action_result)

seg = current_frame.segmentation

print("\n=== ALL RELEVANT ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('N', 'c') and n['pixels'] < 50:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} pixels={n['pixels']}")
    if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 100:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  White: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)} pixels={n['pixels']}")

if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('done'):
    print("LEVEL COMPLETED!")
elif last_action_result.get('game_over'):
    print("Game over")
else:
    print("Level active")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
